summary: Iteration 47 — Full regression of unified AddInterventionModal (InterventionsPage + StudentProfilePage). Backend pytest 4/4 pass (POST /api/interventions persists frequency, GET /api/appointments/ongoing returns frequency). Frontend Playwright flow verified end-to-end: typeahead, grouped optgroups, Daily/Weekly + custom frequency combined as 'Daily · Weekly · 3× per week', locked student on profile page, form reset on reopen, and frequency pill rendering on Appointments Ongoing (69 pills found with data-testid=frequency-<intervention_id>). Intervention created from the locked-student profile modal rendered correctly on the profile Interventions tab as 'Frequency: Weekly · Locked test from profile'. No bugs found.
critical_code_review_comments: AddInterventionModal correctly gates Professionals/Staff optgroups on non-empty lists, but Wellbeing/Leadership have a fallback option that renders even when the list is empty (good — admins can still assign team-wide). Consider the same fallback for Professionals and Staff or document that those groups only appear once users with those roles exist.

POST /api/interventions requires both start_date and review_date (Pydantic Intervention model). AddInterventionModal does NOT mark these as required client-side (canSave only checks student_id + intervention_type + assigned_staff). If a user saves without dates the backend returns 422 and the parent surfaces an error — recommend adding date validation in canSave or marking the <input type=date> as required so the UX matches backend contract.
Frequency normalisation: the modal joins Daily/Weekly/custom with ' · ' separator. Backend stores as-is. Good — single source of truth. No truncation or case issues observed.

/api/appointments/ongoing correctly surfaces `frequency` (line 243) and AppointmentsPage renders [data-testid=frequency-<id>] when non-empty (line 530). Verified with 69 pills in the current demo dataset.

success_rate.backend: 100% (4/4 pytest: login, create with frequency, ongoing includes frequency, empty-frequency round trip)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (modal open, typeahead filter 'emma' → 5 matches, select → dropdown hides, optgroups present (Group/Wellbeing/Leadership; Professionals & Staff absent because demo tenant has 0 users in those roles — expected), frequency combined preview works, save + modal close, form resets on reopen, profile page locked mode hides typeahead, save from profile renders on the profile Interventions tab with correct frequency)

seed_data_creation: Created 2 TEST_ interventions via UI and pytest fixtures. All deleted in test teardown + manual curl cleanup. DB left in original state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Demo tenant /api/users returns only one admin user — there are no users with role 'wellbeing', 'leadership', 'teacher', 'screener', or 'professional'. So the AddInterventionModal's Professionals and Staff optgroups don't render (they only render when their user list is non-empty). Wellbeing/Leadership optgroups DO render because the component has an explicit fallback option that shows even when the list is empty (lines 245-262 of AddInterventionModal.jsx). If a future test wants to exercise the user-level Professionals/Staff options, seed users with those roles first. Also note: POST /api/interventions requires start_date AND review_date (HTTP 422 otherwise) — tests must include those fields. Testids used: new-intervention-btn (InterventionsPage), add-intervention-btn (StudentProfilePage → Interventions tab), add-intervention-modal, intervention-student-typeahead, student-typeahead-dropdown, student-option-<id>, intervention-type-input, intervention-assigned-staff, freq-daily, freq-weekly, freq-custom, save-intervention-btn, frequency-<intervention_id>.
rca of the issue: N/A — all claimed features work as specified.
